options.name: -help
options.description: Prints out the command usage

options.name: -reset
options.description: Resets all or specified options to default value

options.name: -addPhysicalCell
options.description: Add physical cells into hierarchical modules, or as top level cells (flat)

options.name: -backsideBottomRoutingLayer
options.description: Specifies the highest backside lef layer name for global and detail routing.

options.name: -backsideTopRoutingLayer
options.description: Specifies the lowest backside lef layer name for global and detail routing.

options.name: -bottomRoutingLayer
options.description: Specifies the lowest LEF layer name or layer number for global and detail routing. Layer number is from the LEF layer sequence. For example, 2 is equivalent to the second routing layer defined in LEF.

options.name: -compressedPGDB
options.description: Specifies whether the compressed pg feature is enabled. When it's true, reastoreDesign will automatically compress the PG data when load the design. You can use "dbGet top.isPGCompressed" to check if PG in this design is compressed.

options.name: -congEffort
options.description: Specify congestion effort level

options.name: -dual_rail_via_pitch
options.description: <min_via_pitch> <min_filler_via_pitch> <cell_boundary_spacing> Min stacked-via pitch for dual std-cell power-rails on first and third routing layers. Space separated min stacked-via pitch for dual std-cell power-rails on first and third routing layers.

options.name: -earlyClockFlow
options.description: Enable early clock flow

options.name: -earlyPBAMode
options.description: Specify early PBA mode

options.name: -expressRoute
options.description: With "-flowEffort express", should routing-stage be forced.

options.name: -flowEffort
options.description: Specify flow effort level

options.name: -idealHoldFixing
options.description: Enable ideal hold fixing flow

options.name: -ignore_followpin_vias
options.description: if true, ignore followpin vias during detailed placement, optimization and routing.

options.name: -merge_trim_shapes
options.description: <{{layers <layer_name_list> gap <floating> max_merge_num <int> masks {mask_num ...}} ...}> Specify the setting for trim shapes merge. layers, specify the trim layer list which need merge trim shapes. gap, the trim shapes when end to end gap is less the value. max_trim_num, specify the max trim shapes number are merged. masks, specify the trim shapes color need to be merged.

options.name: -node
options.description: {N22|N12|N10|N7|N7Plus|N6|N5|N5PlusPlus|N4|N3|N3E|N2|A16|A14|A12|S11|S10|SF8|SF7|SF5|SF4|SF3|SF2|SF1.4|G7|G5|ICF|I7|I5|P1222|P1276|P1277|P1278|P1278x|P1280|20A|18A|C12|C7|unspecified} set design tech node

options.name: -optimizationDensityScreenMargin
options.description: <double> Increase a margin on top of original density screen for optimization

options.name: -pessimisticMode
options.description: Enable pessimistic mode

options.name: -powerEffort
options.description: Specify power effort level

options.name: -slackWeighting
options.description: {unityWeighting|viewBasedWeighting} Specify slack weighting to be used in implementation flow

options.name: -topRoutingLayer
options.description: Specifies the highest LEF layer name or layer number for global and detail routing. Layer number is from the LEF layer sequence. For example, 2 is equivalent to the second routing layer defined in LEF.

options.name: -trim_grid_group
options.description: Specifies the GROUP name of which set of trim metal grid to be used for placement and routing. See the LEF documentation on the TRIMMETALTRACK keyword for more details.
